title: Contributing to obisindicators
text: outlines propose change obisindicators. detailed info contributing , tidyverse packages, please see development contributing guide.

title: Contributing to obisindicators
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to obisindicators
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing to obisindicators
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("marinebon/obisindicators", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to obisindicators
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to obisindicators
text: Please note obisindicators project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: H3 grids and deckgl
text: Use 1 million records subsampled full OBIS dataset otherwise available https://obis.org/data/access.
code: occ <- obisindicators::occ_1M  # global 1M records subset

title: H3 grids and deckgl
code: hex_res <- 1  # hex_res 0 is too big to work, all others work hex <- obisindicators::make_hex_res(hex_res) # mapview::mapview(hex)  # show the hex grid with h3 IDs  # === Then assign cell numbers to the occurrence data: occ <- occ %>%    mutate(     cell = h3::geo_to_h3(       data.frame(decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude),       res = hex_res))

title: H3 grids and deckgl
text: following function calculates number records, species richness, Simpson index, Shannon index, Hurlbert index (n = 50), Hill numbers cell. Perform calculation species level data: Add cell geometries indicators table (idx):
code: idx <- obisindicators::calc_indicators(occ) grid <- hex %>%    inner_join(     idx,     by = c("hexid" = "cell")) # you can now visualize with: # plot(grid["es"]) # mapview::mapview(grid["es"])

title: H3 grids and deckgl
text: Let’s look resulting indicators map form.
code: obisindicators::gmap_indicator(   grid, "es", label = "ES(50)",   crs="+proj=robin +lon_0=0 +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs")

title: H3 grids and deckgl
text: crazycapivara/h3-r: R bindings H3, hierarchical hexagonal geospatial indexing system Introduction H3 R • H3 R

title: H3 grids and deckgl
text: Introduction r-deckgl • deckgl R Add h3 cluster layer deckgl widget — add_h3_cluster_layer • deckgl R H3: Area Variation / Nick Rabinowitz / Observable Mapbox Utils / Nick Rabinowitz / Observable
code: remotes::install_github("crazycapivara/deckgl") librarian::shelf(   deckgl, htmlwidgets)  ## @knitr h3-cluster-layer data_url <- paste0(   "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uber-common/deck.gl-data/",   "master/website/sf.h3clusters.json") # sample_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(data_url, simplifyDataFrame = FALSE) sample_data <- data_url  properties <- list(   stroked = TRUE,   filled = TRUE,   extruded = FALSE,   getHexagons = ~hexIds,   getFillColor = JS("d => [255, (1 - d.mean / 500) * 255, 0]"),   getLineColor = c(255, 255, 255),   lineWidthMinPixels = 2,   getTooltip = ~mean)  deck <- deckgl(zoom = 10.5, pitch = 20) %>%   add_h3_cluster_layer(     data = sample_data, properties = properties) %>%   add_basemap()  if (interactive())   deck

title: Serving OBIS indicators as H3 hexagon tiles (h3t)
text: build_obis_h3_duckdb() streams OBIS open-data geoparquet via DuckDB httpfs (byte-range, full download), bins occurrences H3, writes two layers: idx_h3 — precomputed -taxa indicators (ES50, Shannon, Simpson, richness, n) resolutions 1–7 (fast default tile layers). occ_h3 — species-level counts resolution tiers 3/5/7, --fly taxon/year-filtered queries. indicator math SQL translation [calc_indicators()] (esn = 50), pinned R reference package tests. can also build small local store shipped data develop :
code: # a demo region first (cheap) — South Atlantic, matching occ_SAtlantic build_obis_h3_duckdb(   src         = "s3://obis-open-data/occurrence/*.parquet",   path_duckdb = "/share/data/obis/obis_h3.duckdb",   region_bbox = c(-69.6008, -60, 20.0091, 0.0751))  # lon_min,lat_min,lon_max,lat_max # the full global build (expensive S3 scan); drop region_bbox build_obis_h3_duckdb(   src         = "s3://obis-open-data/occurrence/*.parquet",   path_duckdb = "/share/data/obis/obis_h3_global.duckdb") build_obis_h3_duckdb(occ_SAtlantic, "obis_h3.duckdb")

title: Serving OBIS indicators as H3 hexagon tiles (h3t)
text: MST server store registered h3t service (H3T_DBS=obis:/share/data/obis/obis_h3.duckdb) fronted Varnish + Caddy:
code: cd /share/github/MarineSensitivity/server docker compose up -d --build h3t h3tcache   # -> https://h3t.marinesensitivity.org

title: Serving OBIS indicators as H3 hexagon tiles (h3t)
text: obis_h3t_sql() generates validated SELECT. filter reads fast precomputed layer; taxon/years filter computes indicator live species store. obis_h3t_url() base64-encodes SQL ?q= tile URL (safely percent-encodes , +// base64 survive query parsing): Inspect value distribution (color-ramp breaks) stats endpoint:
code: library(obisindicators) #> Warning: replacing previous import 'h3::compact' by 'purrr::compact' when #> loading 'obisindicators'  # default ES50 (all taxa) — precomputed, fast obis_h3t_sql("es") #> [1] "SELECT cell_id, es AS value, n FROM idx_h3 WHERE res = LEAST({{res}}, 7)"  # species richness for seabirds since 2000 — computed on the fly cat(obis_h3t_sql("sp", taxon = list(class = "Aves"), years = c(2000, NA))) #> WITH src AS ( #>   SELECT CAST(h3_cell_to_parent(cell_id, LEAST({{res}}, CASE WHEN {{res}} <= 3 THEN 3 WHEN {{res}} <= 5 THEN 5 ELSE 7 END)) AS BIGINT) AS cell_id, #>          species, SUM(records) AS ni #>   FROM occ_h3 #>   WHERE res = CASE WHEN {{res}} <= 3 THEN 3 WHEN {{res}} <= 5 THEN 5 ELSE 7 END #>     AND "class" = 'Aves' #>         AND date_year >= 2000 #>   GROUP BY 1, 2) #> SELECT cell_id, COUNT(*) AS value, SUM(ni) AS n FROM src GROUP BY cell_id obis_h3t_url(   base_url  = "h3tiles://h3tcache.marinesensitivity.org/h3t/{z}/{x}/{y}.h3t",   indicator = "es",   release   = "v20260623") #> [1] "h3tiles://h3tcache.marinesensitivity.org/h3t/{z}/{x}/{y}.h3t?q=U0VMRUNUIGNlbGxfaWQsIGVzIEFTIHZhbHVlLCBuIEZST00gaWR4X2gzIFdIRVJFIHJlcyA9IExFQVNUKHt7cmVzfX0sIDcp&release=v20260623" u <- obis_h3t_url(   base_url  = "https://h3tcache.marinesensitivity.org/h3t/stats",   indicator = "es") jsonlite::fromJSON(u)  # {min, max, p02, p98, n}

title: Serving OBIS indicators as H3 hexagon tiles (h3t)
text: Swap indicator = "sp" richness, add taxon/years filter drive dropdown — choice rebuilds SQL encoded tile URL, authoritative store answers every query.
code: library(mapgl)  tiles_es50 <- obis_h3t_url(   base_url  = "h3tiles://h3tcache.marinesensitivity.org/h3t/{z}/{x}/{y}.h3t",   indicator = "es",   release   = "v20260623")  maplibre(center = c(-25, -30), zoom = 3) |>   add_h3t_source(id = "obis_es50", tiles = tiles_es50) |>   add_fill_layer(     id           = "obis_es50",     source       = "obis_es50",     source_layer = "obis_es50",     fill_color   = interpolate(       column = "value", values = c(1, 50),       stops  = c("#440154", "#21908C", "#FDE725")),  # viridis     fill_opacity = 0.8)

title: obisindicators
text: following function calculates number records, species richness, Simpson index, Shannon index, Hurlbert index (n = 50), Hill numbers cell. Perform calculation species level data: Add cell geometries indicators table (idx):
code: idx <- calc_indicators(occ) grid <- hex %>%    inner_join(     idx,     by = c("hexid" = "cell")) # you could now visualize with: # plot(grid["es"]) # mapview::mapview(grid["es"])

title: obisindicators
text: Let’s look resulting indicators map form.
code: # ES(50) gmap_indicator(grid, "es", label = "ES(50)") # Shannon index gmap_indicator(grid, "shannon", label = "Shannon index") # Number of records, log10 scale, Robinson projection (default) gmap_indicator(grid, "n", label = "# of records", trans = "log10") # Number of records, log10 scale, Geographic projection gmap_indicator(grid, "n", label = "# of records", trans = "log10", crs=4326)

title: resolution
code: occ <- occ_SAtlantic # occ_1M OR occ_SAtlantic

what: Create function to make grid, calculate metrics, and plot maps for different resolution grid sizes
title: resolution
code: res_changes <- function(resolution = 2){   hex_res <- 1  # hex_res 0 is too big to work, all others work   hex <- obisindicators::make_hex_res(resolution)    # === Then assign cell numbers to the occurrence data:   occ <- occ %>%      mutate(       cell = h3::geo_to_h3(         data.frame(decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude),         res = resolution))   idx <- calc_indicators(occ)    grid <- hex %>%      inner_join(       idx,       by = c("hexid" = "cell"))    gmap_indicator(grid, "es", label = "ES(50)") }

title: resolution
text: Details H3 resolution differences can found h3geo docs. Resolutions range 0 (largest) 15 (smallest). Generally, resolution 0 big useful… even functional, sometimes.   resolution S Atlantic completely covered, meaning every hex enough data compute ES(50) diversity metric. can see basic expected patterns : * higher diversity near coast * higher diversity near equator  resolution see gaps throughout central South Atlantic. hexagons enough occurrence records calculate diversity metric. higher resolution, gaps dominate map. places relatively dense surveying efforts enough data calculate diversity metric. Note relatively data-poor center relatively stark boundary spanning southern tip Africa across. boundary visible diversity metric plots lower resolution form high-low diversity boundary. appearance abrupt high-low diversity boundary likely artifact data-poor central South Atlantic . ES50 diversity metric bias data-poor -diverse extremely low amounts data. noted, however, bias much less intense data-poor less-diverse diversity metrics.
code: res_changes(0) #> Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0. #> ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`. #> ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the obisindicators package. #>   Please report the issue at #>   <https://github.com/marinebon/obisindicators/issues>. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated. res_changes(1) res_changes(2) res_changes(3)

what: Build the OBIS H3 DuckDB store — build_obis_h3_duckdb
title: Build the OBIS H3 DuckDB store — build_obis_h3_duckdb
text: Reads OBIS occurrences, bins H3 cells, writes authoritative DuckDB file two layers consumed h3t tile service: idx_h3(res, cell_id, n, sp, shannon, simpson, es) — precomputed -taxa indicators resolutions 1-7 (fast default tile layers). occ_h3(res, cell_id, aphiaid, phylum, class, "order", family, genus, species, date_year, records) — species-level counts resolution tiers 3/5/7 --fly taxon/year-filtered queries.

title: Build the OBIS H3 DuckDB store — build_obis_h3_duckdb
code: build_obis_h3_duckdb(   src,   path_duckdb,   region_bbox = NULL,   esn = 50L,   s3_region = "us-east-1",   s3_anonymous = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Build the OBIS H3 DuckDB store — build_obis_h3_duckdb
text: src occurrence source. Either data.frame occurrences, character vector parquet path(s)/glob(s) readable DuckDB (e.g. "s3://obis-open-data/occurrence/*.parquet"). Must expose columns decimalLongitude, decimalLatitude, species (optionally) aphiaid, phylum, class, order, family, genus, date_year, records, dropped, absence. Missing taxonomic columns filled NULL. path_duckdb output DuckDB file path. region_bbox optional c(lon_min, lat_min, lon_max, lat_max) restrict region (recommended first/demo build). esn expected sample size ES(n); default 50 (matches ES50). s3_region AWS region s3:// sources (default "us-east-1"). s3_anonymous use anonymous S3 access public buckets (default TRUE). overwrite overwrite existing path_duckdb (default TRUE).

title: Build the OBIS H3 DuckDB store — build_obis_h3_duckdb
text: path_duckdb, invisibly.

title: Build the OBIS H3 DuckDB store — build_obis_h3_duckdb
text: indicator math (ES50, Shannon, Simpson, richness) SQL translation calc_indicators() (esn = 50 default), validated package tests.

what: Calculate Biodiversity Indicators, including ES50 (Hurlbert index) — calc_indicators
title: Calculate Biodiversity Indicators, including ES50 (Hurlbert index) — calc_indicators
text: Calculate expected number marine species random sample 50 individuals (records)

title: Calculate Biodiversity Indicators, including ES50 (Hurlbert index) — calc_indicators
code: calc_indicators(df, esn = 50)

title: Calculate Biodiversity Indicators, including ES50 (Hurlbert index) — calc_indicators
text: df data frame unique species observations containing columns: cell, species, records esn expected number marine species

title: Calculate Biodiversity Indicators, including ES50 (Hurlbert index) — calc_indicators
text: Data frame following extra columns: n: number records sp: species richness shannon: Shannon index simpson: Simpson index es: Hurlbert index (n = 50), .e. expected species 50 samples ES(50) hill_1: Hill number exp(shannon) hill_2: Hill number 1/simpson hill_inf: Hill number 1/maxp

title: Calculate Biodiversity Indicators, including ES50 (Hurlbert index) — calc_indicators
text: expected number marine species random sample 50 individuals (records) indicator marine biodiversity richness. ES50 defined OBIS sum(esi) species following per species calculation: `n - ni >= 50 (n total number records cell ni total number records ith-species) esi = 1 - exp(lngamma(n-ni+1) + lngamma(n-50+1) - lngamma(n-ni-50+1) - lngamma(n+1)) n >= 50 - esi = 1 else - esi = NULL Warning: ES50 assumes individuals randomly distributed, sample size sufficiently large, samples taxonomically similar, samples taken manner.

what: Statically map indicators using ggplot — gmap_indicator

text: Statically map indicators using ggplot

title: Statically map indicators using ggplot — gmap_indicator
code: gmap_indicator(   grid,   column = "shannon",   label = "Shannon index",   trans = "identity",       crs = "+proj=robin +lon_0=0 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs" )

title: Statically map indicators using ggplot — gmap_indicator
text: grid spatial features, e.g. hexagons, plot; requires geometry spatial column column column name indicator; default="shannon" label label show legend trans continuous scales, name transformation object object . Built-transformations include "asn", "atanh", "boxcox", "date", "exp", "hms", "identity" (default), "log", "log10", "log1p", "log2", "logit", "modulus", "probability", "probit", "pseudo_log", "reciprocal", "reverse", "sqrt" "time". See ggplot2::continuous_scale crs coordinate reference system; see sf::st_crs()

title: Statically map indicators using ggplot — gmap_indicator

what: Build an h3t tile SQL query for an OBIS biodiversity indicator — obis_h3t_sql
title: Build an h3t tile SQL query for an OBIS biodiversity indicator — obis_h3t_sql
text: Generates read-SELECT (projecting exactly cell_id, value, n) h3t service base64-decodes, validates, executes per tile. {{res}} substituted server-side tile's H3 resolution.

title: Build an h3t tile SQL query for an OBIS biodiversity indicator — obis_h3t_sql
code: obis_h3t_sql(   indicator = c("es", "sp", "shannon", "n"),   taxon = NULL,   years = NULL,   esn = 50L,   res_placeholder = "{{res}}" )

title: Build an h3t tile SQL query for an OBIS biodiversity indicator — obis_h3t_sql
text: indicator one "es" (ES50), "sp" (richness), "shannon", "n" (# records). taxon optional named list/vector restricting taxa, names among phylum, class, order, family, genus, species, e.g. list(class = "Aves") list(phylum = c("Mollusca", "Cnidaria")). years optional c(min, max) year range (either may NA). esn expected sample size ES(n); default 50. res_placeholder resolution placeholder; default "{{res}}".

title: Build an h3t tile SQL query for an OBIS biodiversity indicator — obis_h3t_sql
text: single-line-friendly SQL string.

title: Build an h3t tile SQL query for an OBIS biodiversity indicator — obis_h3t_sql
text: taxon/years filter query reads precomputed idx_h3 layer (fast). filter computes indicator fly species-level occ_h3 store, selecting resolution tier matches tile zoom.

what: Assemble an h3t tile (or stats) URL for an OBIS indicator — obis_h3t_url
title: Assemble an h3t tile (or stats) URL for an OBIS indicator — obis_h3t_url
text: Base64-encodes SQL obis_h3t_sql() ?q= parameter.

title: Assemble an h3t tile (or stats) URL for an OBIS indicator — obis_h3t_url
code: obis_h3t_url(   base_url = "https://h3tcache.marinesensitivity.org/h3t/{z}/{x}/{y}.h3t",   sql = NULL,   release = NULL,   db = NULL,   ... )

title: Assemble an h3t tile (or stats) URL for an OBIS indicator — obis_h3t_url
text: base_url tile URL template, e.g. "https://h3tcache.marinesensitivity.org/h3t/{z}/{x}/{y}.h3t", stats endpoint ".../h3t/stats". mapgl::add_h3t_source() use h3tiles:// scheme. sql SQL obis_h3t_sql() (pass ... build ). release optional release tag appended &release=. db optional &db= registry name (default server uses obis). ... passed obis_h3t_sql() sql missing.

title: Assemble an h3t tile (or stats) URL for an OBIS indicator — obis_h3t_url
text: URL string ?q=<base64> query embedded.

title: obisindicators 0.2.0
text: build_obis_h3_duckdb() builds authoritative DuckDB store OBIS open-data parquet — precomputed -taxa indicator layer (idx_h3, res 1–7) plus species-level store (occ_h3, tiers 3/5/7) --fly taxon/year-filtered queries. ES50/Shannon/Simpson/richness math SQL translation calc_indicators(), pinned new parity test. obis_h3t_sql() / obis_h3t_url() compose validated read-SELECT base64-encoded tile URL h3t service (MarineSensitivity/server/h3t) mapgl::add_h3t_source(). canonical SQL inst/sql/; server build driver data-raw/build_obis_h3_duckdb.R.

title: obisindicators 0.0.2
text: calc_es50() -> calc_indicators() gmap_metric() -> gmap_indicator() Fixed gmap_indicator() use default Robinson projection. Updated vignettes name changes use “indicators” “metrics”. Supplemented documentation calc_indicators() expected input output columns data frame. Added documentation occ_fk occ_1960s occ_2010s Made generation occ_* datasets reproducible set.seed() sampled versions dataset minimize file size Github data-raw/occ.R.

title: obisindicators 0.0.1
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
